summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that Flagr runs with authentication disabled by default, allowing unauthenticated administrative operations including flag creation and deletion.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should allow creating and deleting flags via the administrative API without any authentication, demonstrating complete auth bypass.
actual_observed_result: Successfully created flag ID 1 without authentication via POST /api/v1/flags, and deleted it via DELETE /api/v1/flags/1 without authentication. The default configuration has both JWT_AUTH_ENABLED and BASIC_AUTH_ENABLED set to false.
verification_method: The run script creates a flag via unauthenticated POST request, verifies the flag was created (proof of administrative access), then deletes it via unauthenticated DELETE request. Success is confirmed by checking for valid JSON response with flag ID.
evidence_produced_by_run: Flag creation succeeded without authentication: POST /api/v1/flags returned valid flag object with ID
Flag deletion succeeded without authentication: DELETE /api/v1/flags/{id} returned success
No authentication headers were sent in requests

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository from GitHub on the host, builds the Docker image, and runs the container.
reproduction.pinned_commit_or_ref: current HEAD of openflagr/flagr main branch
